title: Prosalegenden
summary: The General Corpus XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan CME item CME00061, which encodes Carl Horstmann's complete article 'Prosalegenden' from Anglia 3 (1880), pp. 293-360. Horstmann edited a heterogeneous selection: William Caxton's 1484 Life and Translation of Saint Winifred from the imperfect early print bound in Lambeth Palace Library MS 306, collated against the British Museum copy now BL C.10.b.19; John Mirk's Winifred sermon from BL Cotton Claudius A II with three short supplies or readings from Gonville and Caius MS 168/89; and Marian legends, a Life of Saint Dorothy, and a Life and Miracles of Saint Jerome from Lambeth MS 432. Complete whole-object caches retain all 618 canvases of BSB's Anglia 3 volume and all 415 canvases of Lambeth MS 306, mapping the article and bound Caxton print inside them. No public whole-object facsimile was found for MS 432, Cotton Claudius A II, Caius MS 168/89, or Horstmann's BL Caxton collation copy; mediated requests are therefore directed to the complete physical objects rather than cropped work portions.
review_notes: The immediate edition is Anglia 3 (1880), not Anglia 8. Its article is signed by Horstmann at Munster on printed p. 360.

Horstmann's p. 320 prose says MS Lambeth 402, but the section headings repeatedly say 432 and the current Lambeth MS 432 catalog exactly matches the three contents; MS 402 is unrelated. The lineage resolves the witness to MS 432 while preserving the error.
Horstmann labels the Marian collection 'fol. 95'; Lambeth's current content record places it at f. 85. The discrepancy is retained as an open locator question.

On printed pp. 314-316, the Caxton Latin office continues in the lower page area while the Mirk addendum begins above it. The XML serializes both streams and therefore repeats PB references 314-316; this is not accidental duplicate pagination.

The Caxton unit in MS 306 is a bound early print, not a manuscript witness. It is textually incomplete because its final leaf is missing even though the cached surrogate completely covers the provider's current 415-canvas physical object.
The former Earl of Dysart copy is now Morgan PML 35083. Morgan records purchase through Quaritch from Sotheby's Ham House sale, lot 319, 20 June 1938.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00061
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by the repository publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00061
entities.description: Public encoding of Horstmann's article, including German commentary, Middle English prose, Latin offices and prayers, footnotes, page breaks, line-number milestones, and italic expansion or supply typography.

entities.label: Carl Horstmann's Prosalegenden (1880)
entities.description: The immediate scholarly source of CME00061, published as a complete article in Anglia 3, with German introductions and apparatus around transcriptions of several Middle English hagiographic texts.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, 'Prosalegenden,' Anglia: Zeitschrift fur englische Philologie 3 (1880), 293-360 (Halle a. S.: Max Niemeyer, 1880).
bibliographic.title: Prosalegenden

entities.label: BSB complete digital volume Anglia 3 (1880)
entities.description: The complete 618-canvas BSB digital object for Anglia 3. Horstmann's article occupies canvases 303-370, corresponding to printed pp. 293-360.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and 618 color JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: William Caxton's Life and Translation of Saint Winifred (1484)
entities.description: A sixteen-leaf folio incunable in Middle English, translated and printed by William Caxton at Westminster in 1484. Current cataloging describes it as an abridged translation of Robert of Shrewsbury's Vita; Caxton's exact physical source exemplar remains unidentified.

bibliographic.citation: The life of the holy and blessed virgin Saint Winifred; Add: Translation of Saint Winifred (Westminster: William Caxton, 1484), ISTC iw00062000, STC 25853.
bibliographic.title: The life of the holy and blessed virgin Saint Winifred. Add: Translation of Saint Winifred

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 306
entities.description: A composite manuscript codex of ff. i+204, bound c. 1504-1512, which contains a separately produced Caxton printed unit on bound ff. 188r-201v. The codex entity is retained because the public surrogate covers the whole physical volume.

physical_description.support: Paper codex with a paper incunable component
physical_description.extent: ff. i+204; Caxton print at ff. 188r-201v
notes: The Caxton component lacks its final printed leaf.
Bound ff. 194 and 195 are reversed relative to the text's logical order.

entities.label: Caxton Winifred copy bound in Lambeth MS 306
entities.description: The imperfect Caxton print cataloged as item 27 in MS 306, bound at ff. 188r-201v. It was Horstmann's direct copy text; its final leaf is missing and bound ff. 194 and 195 must be read in reverse physical order.

entities.label: Lambeth complete public digital sequence for MS 306
entities.description: All 415 provider canvases, from front cover through spine. The bound Caxton unit is mapped at canvases 379-406, with a separately recorded logical reading order.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and 415 color JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: British Library C.10.b.19 / IB.55109
entities.description: The near-complete Caxton copy, lacking the blank, that Horstmann explicitly used for variant readings under references to the British Museum exemplar. Horstmann printed the obsolete or erroneous shelfmark C.11.b.11; current ISTC and historical census evidence identify C.10.b.19 / IB.55109.

holding.notes: Horstmann's shelfmark does not match current copy-level authorities.

entities.label: Morgan Library PML 35083
entities.description: The complete sixteen-leaf Caxton copy with blank, formerly at Ham House in the Earl of Dysart's library. Morgan records its purchase in 1938 through Quaritch after Sotheby's lot 319.

notes: Morgan corrects de Ricci's Rawlinson provenance identification and provides the 1938 transfer trail.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Claudius A II
entities.description: A 158-folio parchment codex. Its principal item, ff. 3-156, contains John Mirk's Festial and other pastoral material; Horstmann transcribed the Winifred sermon beginning at f. 80v.

date_statements.label: Festial component
date_statements.value: Second quarter of the fifteenth century

physical_description.extent: 158 folios; Festial ff. 3v-125v; Winifred sermon begins f. 80v

entities.label: Gonville and Caius College MS 168/89
entities.description: A fifteenth-century paper Festial manuscript of 168 folios. Horstmann used it only to supply one Cotton lacuna and record two short variant readings in the Winifred sermon.

holding.notes: The combined modern form 168/89 is confirmed by the U-M Middle English Bibliography; James prints 168 and 89 as paired catalog numbers.

notes: 31 lines per page; given by William Moore.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 432
alternate_labels: LIFE OF S. JEROME. HAMPOLE ETC.
entities.description: A fifteenth-century paper codex of ff. i+95. Horstmann transcribed the Life and Miracles of Saint Jerome at ff. 1-37, fifteen Marian miracles beginning f. 85, and the Life of Saint Dorothy at ff. 90-94.

notes: 29 lines per page in a clear current hand.
The final quire lacks its twelfth leaf and preserves only a fragment of leaf eleven.

entities.label: Robert of Shrewsbury's Vita Sanctae Wenefredae
entities.description: The twelfth-century Latin life identified by modern incunable cataloging as the source tradition abridged and translated by Caxton. The exact physical exemplar used by Caxton is not identified.

subject.description: Caxton Life, Translation, and associated Latin office on pp. 295-316

assertion.notes: Footnotes explicitly cite readings of the British Museum exemplar.
This records Horstmann's enumeration of the copy, not evidence that he collated it.
The textual source tradition is supported, but Caxton's exact exemplar remains unidentified.

locators: starts f. 80v; terminal folio not established

subject.description: One supplied Cotton lacuna and two short variant notes in the Winifred sermon

object.description: Corresponding Festial readings; exact Caius pages not identified by Horstmann

subject.description: Marian legends, Dorothy, and Jerome, pp. 320-360

locators: Marian miracles ff. 85r-89v according to current catalog sequence

assertion.notes: The article's isolated references to MS 402 and f. 95 are treated as errors, not alternate witnesses.

notes: Canvas 378 precedes the print; canvas 407 is blank f. 202r and canvas 408 begins later manuscript matter.
The bundle is complete for the current physical object even though the historic printed copy lacks its final leaf.
Canvases 1-414 use the 1800-pixel-wide derivative; the narrow spine at canvas 415 uses native full size.

access.access_method: An exact official LUNA search returned zero results; do not infer permanent non-digitization.

local_copies.label: Lambeth LUNA exact MS432 search result
notes: The cached response records totalResults=0; it is not a manuscript facsimile.

access.access_method: Email the completed photography form to request a complete-object reproduction: covers/boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, and every folio of MS 432. State the target work loci separately.
notes: The ordinary form lists a 40-scan maximum; a complete 95-folio codex plus bindings therefore requires prior discussion.
Ask separately about redistribution or print-on-demand use.

access.access_method: Ask Lambeth about MS Film 1226 or World Microfilms Section I, reel 9; coverage should be confirmed before treating either as a whole-codex surrogate.
Use the BL digitisation form and request the complete physical object, including binding, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, and all 158 folios; identify the Winifred sermon beginning f. 80v separately.
Email the library to confirm current shelfmark and ask whether a complete-object color facsimile can be supplied, including covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, and all 168 folios.

local_copies.label: Complete volume 1 of James's Caius manuscript catalogue
Complete de Ricci Census of Caxtons

access.access_method: ISTC links a former BL viewer object, but access.bl.uk failed DNS and tested replacement endpoints did not deliver the manifest.
Request a complete-object reproduction of shelfmark C.10.b.19, including binding, pastedowns, flyleaves, blank surfaces, and every printed leaf; do not request only variant passages.

Use the photography form to request the complete PML 35083 physical object, including binding, pastedowns, flyleaves, blank a1, and all printed leaves. Publication use and dissemination require separate details and authorization.

rights.component: 1880 edition text, medieval texts, and editorial matter
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1880; U-M also labels its representation No Copyright-United States.
U-M states No Copyright-United States at collection level.
notes: This is jurisdiction-specific and does not license third-party facsimile images.

rights.component: BSB digital images and IIIF object
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest states No Copyright - Non-Commercial Use Only.
notes: The non-commercial condition prevents assuming that these image derivatives may be bundled into commercial print-on-demand products.

rights.basis: The IIIF metadata gives only LPL as rights value, while Lambeth's forms require separate permission for reproduction or publication.
notes: Keep the complete bundle Git-ignored unless written redistribution permission is obtained.

rights.basis: The order form limits supplied reproductions to the declared use and the permission form governs publication.
BL Imaging Services requires written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material.
Morgan forbids unauthorized duplication or dissemination of its photographic material and requests publication-specific rights information.
Local caching documents research access and does not itself establish redistribution rights.

editorial_practices.description: Horstmann assembled separately transmitted Caxton, Mirk, Marian, Dorothy, and Jerome texts under a new article title; the result is an edited selection, not one medieval compilation.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Caxton overbars, superscript contractions, and inconsistent e-like hooks
editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters are generally italicized; inconsistent hooks on g, d, h, and n are not reproduced
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann explicitly expands Caxton's abbreviation signs and omits an inconsistent class of hooks, so his typography cannot be treated as a facsimile of the print.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Lambeth Caxton copy, British Museum copy, Cotton Festial, Caius Festial, and MS 432
editorial_practices.representation: Copy-specific corrections, variants, supplied words, and manuscript readings appear in footnotes and italics
editorial_practices.description: The apparatus distinguishes the Lambeth print's later corrections, British Museum readings, Cotton omissions, and Caius supplies rather than silently merging them.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Horstmann's italic expansion and supply typography
editorial_practices.representation: 2,372 HI elements with REND='italic'; no ABBR elements
editorial_practices.description: The encoding preserves Horstmann's printed italics at scale but does not reconstruct original abbreviation forms in the early print or manuscripts.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00061.xml.
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00061 and preserves Horstmann's title, complete article text, notes, source labels, page references, typography, and editorial statements.

evidence.citation: Reproducible XML element and attribute audit of CME00061.xml.
evidence.summary: Counts 1 DIV1, 6 DIV2, 23 DIV3, 29 HEAD, 104 P, 1 LG, 2 L, 71 PB, 560 MILESTONE, 126 NOTE, 2,372 italic HI, 4 Q, and no ABBR; PB 314-316 repeat because two page streams overlap.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Prosalegenden, CME00061, and CME collection statement.
evidence.summary: Provides the public text, stable identifier, collection context, and No Copyright-United States assertion.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek IIIF manifest for Anglia 3 (1880), bsb11642505.
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete volume, bibliographic identifiers, shelfmark, 618-canvas inventory, article placement, and manifest-level NoC-NC 1.0 rights statement.

evidence.citation: Visual audit of BSB Anglia 3 canvases 303-370 and representative full-volume surfaces.
evidence.summary: Confirms article boundaries, source headings, the pp. 314-316 two-stream layout, MS 432 headings, the erroneous p. 320 MS 402 statement, Horstmann's signature, and sampled full-color 1600-pixel derivatives.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library Archives Catalogue, MS 306, item 27.
evidence.summary: Confirms the codex extent, Caxton print at ff. 188r-201v, STC 25853, composite binding, missing print leaf, and reproduction references.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library LUNA IIIF Presentation manifest for MS 306.
evidence.summary: Provides the complete provider inventory and exact canvas/image URLs for all exposed surfaces.

evidence.citation: Visual and sequence audit of the complete Lambeth MS 306 IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Confirms binding-to-spine coverage, the printed unit at canvases 379-406, its terminal blank/next matter, and the reversal requiring canvases 393-394 before 391-392 in textual order.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library Archives Catalogue, MS 432, LIFE OF S. JEROME. HAMPOLE ETC.
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, fifteenth-century date, physical description, Jerome ff. 1-37, Marian miracles beginning f. 85, Dorothy ff. 90-94, historic shelfmarks, and microfilm routes.

evidence.citation: Lambeth LUNA exact query for MS432* in the Manuscripts collection.
evidence.summary: The official JSON response returned totalResults=0 on the review date, supporting a dated no-public-image finding rather than a permanent non-digitization claim.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library Digital Photography and Permission to Reproduce forms.
evidence.summary: Documents contact details, formats, fees, ordinary 40-scan limit, private research undertaking, and separate publication permission.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Claudius A II and ff. 3-156.
evidence.summary: Confirms 158-folio whole codex, physical description, Festial ff. 3v-125v, component date 1425-1449, West Midlands origin, and lack of a digitised-content link.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Gonville and Caius College, vol. 1 (1907), p. 191.
evidence.summary: Describes the 168-folio fifteenth-century paper Festial manuscript, C.M.A. 1087, its dimensions, collation, contents, and relationship to Cotton Claudius A II.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Middle English Bibliography, Festial.
evidence.summary: Uses the modern combined shelfmark Cambridge, Gonville and Caius College 168/89 and lists it among Festial manuscripts.

evidence.citation: Gonville and Caius College Library, Visitors and historic collections.
evidence.summary: States that external researchers with a genuine research interest may arrange historic-collection access by emailing library@cai.cam.ac.uk.

evidence.citation: ISTC iw00062000, The life of the holy and blessed virgin Saint Winifred.
evidence.summary: Current authority for 1484 imprint, title, language, references, six holding entries, copy notes, BL digital-resource ARKs, and Robert of Shrewsbury source statement.

evidence.citation: Morgan Library & Museum, PML 35083, The lyf of the holy and blessid vyrgyn saynt Wenefryde.
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete 16-leaf copy, 1484 imprint, binding, corrected provenance, former Dysart ownership, Sotheby's 20 June 1938 lot 319, Quaritch purchase for Morgan, and no public image on the item page.

evidence.citation: Morgan Library & Museum, PML 696, Caxton Winifred leaf a6.
evidence.summary: Distinguishes Morgan's detached leaf a6 from its complete PML 35083 copy and confirms the leaf's Bennett provenance.

evidence.citation: Morgan Library & Museum, Imaging & Rights and Application for Photography.
evidence.summary: Explains purchasable digital images, online request data, processing times, case-by-case new photography, publication details, and the prohibition on unauthorized duplication or dissemination.

evidence.citation: Seymour de Ricci, A Census of Caxtons (1909), entry 100, p. 107.
evidence.summary: Historical copy census documenting the then Dysart, British Museum, Lambeth, Bagford, Ames, Bodleian, and Morgan copies or fragments; later Morgan cataloging corrects part of its first-copy provenance.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Incunable Shelfmarks Concordance, Bod-inc W-024.
evidence.summary: Maps Bod-inc W-024 to current shelfmark Arch. G d.17; ISTC identifies the holding as one leaf.

evidence.citation: Direct delivery tests for ISTC's British Library ARK for C.10.b.19.
evidence.summary: On 2026-07-12 access.bl.uk and api.bl.uk failed DNS, while tested bl.digirati.io forms returned 403; no complete manifest or image sequence was retrievable.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitisation Services and Imaging Services terms.
evidence.summary: Provides the current online order route, required shelfmark/page/format details, fee basis, rare-item photography caveat, color-request instruction, and reproduction-permission restrictions.

open_questions.question: Why does Horstmann print MS 402 in the p. 320 commentary and f. 95 for the Marian legends when the exact matching current record is MS 432 at f. 85?
next_steps: Inspect MS 432 directly or obtain a complete-object facsimile.
Check older Lambeth catalogs and Horstmann's papers for typographical or renumbering evidence.

open_questions.question: Will Lambeth supply and authorize a complete color facsimile of MS 432 rather than only the target folios?
next_steps: Email archives@churchofengland.org before ordering because the complete object exceeds the form's ordinary 40-scan maximum.
Specify covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, all 95 folios, and separate work loci and reuse plans.

open_questions.question: Can complete color facsimiles of Cotton Claudius A II and Caius MS 168/89 be obtained, and what are the exact terminal folio and Caius loci of Horstmann's Winifred readings?
next_steps: Request the entire 158-folio Cotton codex through BL Digitisation Services.
Ask Caius Library to confirm MS 168/89 and quote a complete-object surrogate.
Map the sermon and three Caius-supplied readings after complete images are received.

open_questions.question: Will the former British Library digital object for C.10.b.19 return through a current IIIF endpoint?
next_steps: Recheck the ISTC-linked ARK and BL's current Digitised Manuscripts migration.
If unavailable, order the complete copy including binding and blanks rather than selected variants.

open_questions.question: Which physical Latin exemplar or combination of sources did Caxton abridge and translate?
next_steps: Compare Caxton against the manuscript tradition of Robert of Shrewsbury's Vita and current source scholarship.
Do not identify Cotton Claudius A V or another surviving manuscript as the physical exemplar without provenance evidence.

open_questions.question: May BSB, Lambeth, BL, Morgan, or newly supplied manuscript images be redistributed in the public repository or commercial/noncommercial print-on-demand outputs?
next_steps: Treat each provider and image object separately.
Keep caches Git-ignored and publish links/locators only until written permission or an applicable open license is confirmed.
Note that BSB's exact manifest is NoC-NC and the other providers impose permission conditions.
